title: SonicWall Site-to-Site VPN IKE and Tunnel Down Events
spl:
index=netfw (sourcetype="dell:sonicwall" OR sourcetype="dell:sonicwall:firewall") earliest=-24h
| search (IKE OR "IPSec" OR "VPN" OR "tunnel") AND (down OR failed OR "phase 1" OR "phase 2" OR timeout OR disconnect)
| stats count by host, name, peer, msg
| sort -count
description: Branch and partner connectivity depends on stable SonicWall VPN tunnels. IKE failures, PFS mismatches, or dead peer detection events often indicate credential rotation errors, routing changes, or active interference—each with different response playbooks.
value: Network operations teams track SonicWall site-to-site VPN tunnel health, diagnosing IKE negotiation failures (PSK mismatches, proposal incompatibilities), measuring tunnel uptime against SLA targets, and detecting flapping tunnels that degrade branch connectivity.
implementation: Ingest complete VPN subsystem logs; avoid filtering at the forwarder. Tag each appliance with site metadata. Correlate with certificate expiry and shared-secret change tickets. Tune phrases to your SonicOS version message catalog.
visualization: Timeline (VPN errors), Table (peer gateway, message), Single value (active tunnels down).
cimSpl:
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count

references.title: Splunkbase — Dell SonicWall Add-on for Splunk

knownFalsePositives: Anomalies from new SaaS app rollouts, browser updates, or vendor URL category changes.
grandmaExplanation: Our office locations connect to each other through encrypted tunnels. When a tunnel breaks, the offices cannot communicate. We track every time a tunnel goes down and why, so we can fix it quickly and make sure each link meets its uptime promise.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• SonicWall syslog to Splunk. Data in `index=firewall` with `sourcetype=sonicwall`. Key fields: `msg_id` (VPN-related: 97=IKE Phase 1 failure, 98=IKE Phase 2 failure, 878=VPN Tunnel Status, 537=IKE Notification), `vpn_policy`, `src`, `dst`, `fw_action`, `vpn_status` (tunnel up/down).
• SonicWall S2S VPN uses IKEv1 or IKEv2. IKE Phase 1 failures typically involve pre-shared key mismatch, IKE proposal mismatch (encryption/hash/DH group), or certificate issues. Phase 2 failures typically involve IPSec transform set mismatches or proxy ID mismatches.
• Create a `sonicwall_vpn_tunnels.csv` lookup: `vpn_policy,peer_ip,remote_site_name,business_criticality,sla_target_uptime_pct` mapping tunnel names to business context.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Verify VPN events:
```spl
index=firewall sourcetype=sonicwall earliest=-7d
| where msg_id IN ("97","98","878","537")
| stats count by msg_id
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

Primary search — VPN tunnel status and failures:
```spl
index=firewall sourcetype=sonicwall earliest=-7d
| where msg_id IN ("97","98","878")
| eval event_type=case(msg_id="97", "IKE-Phase1-Fail", msg_id="98", "IKE-Phase2-Fail", msg_id="878", "Tunnel-Status")
| lookup sonicwall_vpn_tunnels.csv vpn_policy OUTPUT remote_site_name business_criticality sla_target_uptime_pct
| timechart span=1h count by event_type
```

Understanding this SPL: Trends IKE Phase 1 and Phase 2 failures alongside tunnel status changes. Clustered Phase 1 failures to one peer suggest a config mismatch or the remote side changed its IKE settings. Phase 2 failures after successful Phase 1 suggest an IPSec transform or proxy ID mismatch. Periodic tunnel-down/up cycles (flapping) indicate DPD (Dead Peer Detection) issues or unstable internet links.

Tunnel uptime calculation:
```spl
index=firewall sourcetype=sonicwall msg_id=878 earliest=-30d
| rex "vpn_policy=\"(?<tunnel>[^\"]+)\""
| eval status=if(match(_raw, "(?i)up"), "up", "down")
| sort tunnel _time
| streamstats current=f last(status) as prev_status last(_time) as prev_time by tunnel
| where status="down" AND prev_status="up"
| eval downtime_min=round((now()-_time)/60, 0)
| stats sum(downtime_min) as total_downtime_min count as outages by tunnel
| eval uptime_pct=round((1 - total_downtime_min/(30*24*60))*100, 2)
| lookup sonicwall_vpn_tunnels.csv vpn_policy as tunnel OUTPUT sla_target_uptime_pct remote_site_name
| eval sla_met=if(uptime_pct >= tonumber(sla_target_uptime_pct), "MET", "MISSED")
| sort uptime_pct
```

Step 3 — Validate
(a) In SonicWall: VPN > Settings > VPN Policies. Compare tunnel status with Splunk.
(b) Temporarily disable a test VPN policy and verify the down event appears.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard: Row 1 — Tunnel status map (all tunnels). Row 2 — IKE failure trend. Row 3 — Uptime SLA compliance.
Alert: Immediate on any business-critical tunnel down. Daily for SLA misses.

Runbook:
1. **Phase 1 failure** — PSK mismatch or IKE proposal mismatch. Coordinate with remote site admin.
2. **Phase 2 failure** — Check IPSec transform set and proxy IDs. Often happens after remote firewall upgrade.
3. **Flapping tunnel** — Check internet link quality. Consider adjusting DPD timers.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **No VPN events** — SonicWall may not be logging VPN events to syslog. Check Log > Categories > VPN.
• **Tunnel status missing** — `msg_id=878` may vary by SonicOS version. Check the syslog reference for your version.
